누가복음 18:1.content: He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray, and not give up,

누가복음 18:2.content: saying, “There was a judge in a certain city who didn’t fear God, and didn’t respect man.

누가복음 18:3.content: A widow was in that city, and she often came to him, saying, ‘Defend me from my adversary!’

누가복음 18:4.content: He wouldn’t for a while, but afterward he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God, nor respect man,

누가복음 18:5.content: yet because this widow bothers me, I will defend her, or else she will wear me out by her continual coming.’ ”

누가복음 18:6.content: The Lord said, “Listen to what the unrighteous judge says.

누가복음 18:7.content: Won’t God avenge his chosen ones who are crying out to him day and night, and yet he exercises patience with them?

누가복음 18:8.content: I tell you that he will avenge them quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?”

누가복음 18:9.content: He spoke also this parable to certain people who were convinced of their own righteousness, and who despised all others.

누가복음 18:10.content: “Two men went up into the temple to pray; one was a Pharisee, and the other was a tax collector.

누가복음 18:11.content: The Pharisee stood and prayed to himself like this: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like the rest of men, extortionists, unrighteous, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.

누가복음 18:12.content: I fast twice a week. I give tithes of all that I get.’

누가복음 18:13.content: But the tax collector, standing far away, wouldn’t even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’

누가복음 18:14.content: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted.”

누가복음 18:15.content: They were also bringing their babies to him, that he might touch them. But when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.

누가복음 18:16.content: Jesus summoned them, saying, “Allow the little children to come to me, and don’t hinder them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

누가복음 18:17.content: Most certainly, I tell you, whoever doesn’t receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”

누가복음 18:18.content: A certain ruler asked him, saying, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”

누가복음 18:19.content: Jesus asked him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good, except one: God.

누가복음 18:20.content: You know the commandments: ‘Don’t commit adultery,’ ‘Don’t murder,’ ‘Don’t steal,’ ‘Don’t give false testimony,’ ‘Honor your father and your mother.’ ”

누가복음 18:21.content: He said, “I have observed all these things from my youth up.”

누가복음 18:22.content: When Jesus heard these things, he said to him, “You still lack one thing. Sell all that you have, and distribute it to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”

누가복음 18:23.content: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was very rich.

누가복음 18:24.content: Jesus, seeing that he became very sad, said, “How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!

누가복음 18:25.content: For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

누가복음 18:26.content: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”

누가복음 18:27.content: But he said, “The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.”

누가복음 18:28.content: Peter said, “Look, we have left everything and followed you.”

누가복음 18:29.content: He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or wife, or brothers, or parents, or children, for God’s Kingdom’s sake,

누가복음 18:30.content: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the world to come, eternal life.”

누가복음 18:31.content: He took the twelve aside, and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all the things that are written through the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be completed.

누가복음 18:32.content: For he will be delivered up to the Gentiles, will be mocked, treated shamefully, and spit on.

누가복음 18:33.content: They will scourge and kill him. On the third day, he will rise again.”

누가복음 18:34.content: They understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they didn’t understand the things that were said.

누가복음 18:35.content: As he came near Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the road, begging.

누가복음 18:36.content: Hearing a multitude going by, he asked what this meant.

누가복음 18:37.content: They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.

누가복음 18:38.content: He cried out, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”

누가복음 18:39.content: Those who led the way rebuked him, that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”

누가복음 18:40.content: Standing still, Jesus commanded him to be brought to him. When he had come near, he asked him,

누가복음 18:41.content: “What do you want me to do?” He said, “Lord, that I may see again.”

누가복음 18:42.content: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you.”

누가복음 18:43.content: Immediately he received his sight and followed him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, praised God.
